Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank Q2 net profit rises 31%
data.description:
By Staff Writer

Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank's (ADCB) second-quarter 2026 net profit jumped 31% year-on-year (YoY) to AED 3.4 billion ($926 million), driven by higher non-interest income and lower impairment charges.

The result topped analysts’ mean estimate of AED2.9 billion, according to LSEG data.

Operating income rose 6% YoY to AED5.9 billion, while operating profit before impairment charges rose 3% to AED4.4 billion.

Net interest and Islamic financing income rose 2% to AED3.7 billion, while non-interest income surged 12% to AED2.3 billion.

Impairment charges declined YoY 55% YoY to AED638 million.

For the first half of 2026, net profit was up 34% to AED6.7 billion from the corresponding period a year earlier.

(Writing by Brinda Darasha; editing by Seban Scaria)

brinda.darasha@lseg.com

data.title: GODIGIT: Gross premium and net earned premium grew, but profit after tax and EPS declined year-over-year
data.description:
Gross premium written rose to ₹273,086 lakhs year-over-year, with net earned premium at ₹200,696 lakhs. Profit after tax declined to ₹8,639 lakhs, and the combined ratio increased to 112.3%. Expenses exceeded regulatory limits, and Ind AS adoption was deferred to FY 2027-28.

Original document: Go Digit General Insurance Limited [GODIGIT] Interim report — Jul. 23 2026
